title: slash_plugin / plugin_marketplace use raw  — quoted args containing spaces break silently
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
kiss
## Location
src/cli/repl/slash.rs:470-511, src/cli/repl/slash.rs:1277-1294
## Violation
Every sub-command parser splits on a single space. Arguments containing spaces — paths, URLs with query strings, marketplace names with hyphens-but-also-spaces — get truncated. Quoted lines:

    fn slash_plugin(args: &str) -> SlashCommandResult {
        let sub_parts: Vec<&str> = args.splitn(2, ' ').collect();
        ...
        let market_parts: Vec<&str> = sub_args.splitn(2, ' ').collect();
        let market_cmd = market_parts.first().copied().unwrap_or("");
        let market_target = market_parts.get(1).copied().unwrap_or("").trim();

`/plugin marketplace add "/Users/me/My Plugins/mp"` becomes target=`"/Users/me/My`. The user is informed only by a generic "Could not resolve" error. The same problem exists in slash_commit / slash_commit_push_pr and slash_continue.
## Mandated refactor
Adopt `shell_words::split` (or `shellwords` / `winsplit` for the cross-platform case) to honour quoted args. Better: model each slash command's args using `clap`'s low-level parser so we get the same UX as the project's own CLI (already a clap consumer per CLAUDE.md). Add a regression test for `/plugin install "path with spaces"`.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
